raw_response:
While both reports provide a strong analysis, Variant A is the clear winner because its specificity translates directly into immediate, high-priority actions. As the operator, the quantitative insight that my signature dish, the Biang Biang noodles, is also the primary subject in 33% of recent negative reviews is a critical fire alarm that requires immediate kitchen intervention. This level of data-driven specificity, combined with actionable feedback on defensive management responses and quality drops for repeat customers, provides a concrete operational to-do list. Variant B offers a superior strategic overview of the market and better source diversity by citing specific blogs and platforms, but its key vulnerability—a "stale soup" complaint—feels less systemic. Variant A's report is a diagnostic tool that empowers me to fix core product and service issues this week, making it fundamentally more useful for the day-to-day reality of running the restaurant.

```json
{"winner": "A", "dimensions": {"coverage": "B", "specificity": "A", "source_diversity": "B", "actionability": "A", "specialist_set_correctness": "TIE"}, "supporting_urls": ["https://www.google.com/maps", "https://www.trojmiasto.pl/", "https://www.kukbuk.pl/", "https://www.wykop.pl/", "https://pyszne.pl/", "https://wolt.com/", "https://wanderlog.com/"]}
```
